name: Rebel Firebrand
oracle_text: Malfunction 2 (This permanent enters tapped with two malfunction counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)\n{1}: Remove a malfunction counter from ~.
flavor_text: The Fist guns can't aim if their hands are burned off.

validation_errors: Line 1: "This permanent" should use "~" or "this" only in specific MTG contexts
Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(This permanent enters tapped with two malfunction counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)"
Malfunction already causes the permanent to enter tapped — explicit 'enters tapped' is redundant

design_notes: WR signpost uncommon for Spark Rebellion archetype. Malfunction 2 provides a significant tempo cost (enters tapped, two-turn clock to be active), justified by aggressive stats (3/2). The activated ability {1} to remove malfunction counters allows interactive play: you can choose to pay mana to accelerate the card's usefulness, or hold up mana for combat tricks. This creates decision-making and rewards tempo planning. The modest mana cost means it can come down turn 2 and be active by turn 4, fitting WR's aggressive tempo play. Flavor reflects the rebel underdog nature and pyromaniac style. Stats fit common power budget: 3+2=5, CMC 3, so P+T = CMC+2, well within acceptable range."
